title: The Reward Model
concept: A separate neural network (the reward model) is trained on human preference data to output a single scalar score representing how good a response is.

exercises.prompt: Why do we need a separate way to grade an AI's responses?

options.text: To make the AI answer questions faster
To give the AI a way to evaluate and score how good its own answers are
To store all human conversations in a database

exercises.explain: To teach an AI to behave safely and helpfully, we need a way to grade its answers so it knows which ones humans prefer.

cards.title: Meet the AI Judge
cards.body: How do we teach an AI to know the difference between a great answer and a mediocre one? We build a second AI whose entire job is to act as a judge, grading every response with a score!

cards.title: What is a Reward Model?
cards.body: A Reward Model is a separate neural network trained to act like a judge. It takes an AI's response and outputs a single number (a score) that represents how much a human would like or approve of that answer.

cards.title: The Gymnastics Judge
cards.body: Think of a gymnastics judge sitting by the mat. The gymnast (our AI writer) does a routine, and the judge (the Reward Model) raises a single card showing a score, like 9.5 out of 10. The judge doesn't write the routine; they just score how well it was performed based on the rules.

cards.title: Scoring a Recipe
cards.body: If a user asks for a cookie recipe, the main AI might write a response. The Reward Model looks at this recipe and outputs a score of +1.8 for a helpful, step-by-step answer, or a score of -2.5 if the response is confusing or unsafe. This score helps the AI learn to write better recipes next time.

cards.title: Two Different Roles
cards.body: People often think the main chatbot and the reward system are the exact same AI. In reality, the Reward Model is a completely separate neural network trained specifically on human feedback to evaluate, not to write.

cards.title: Generator vs. Judge
cards.body: Comparing the different roles of the text generation model and the evaluation model.

left.text: Creates creative drafts and answers to match the user's prompt.

right.text: Judges the drafts and outputs a single score showing how good they are.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary function of a Reward Model?

options.text: It generates new text responses from scratch.
It outputs a single numerical score representing the quality of a response.
It automatically deletes offensive user prompts before they reach the AI.
It translates text into different languages.

exercises.explain: The Reward Model is trained using human preferences to output a single scalar value (a number) that reflects how good or appropriate a response is.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The Reward Model is the AI that actually writes the chatbot's replies to the user.
exercises.explain: The Reward Model does not write the responses; it is a separate network that evaluates the responses generated by the main AI.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe the output of the Reward Model.
exercises.sentence: The Reward Model evaluates an AI's response and outputs a single numerical [blank] to rate its quality.
exercises.explain: The Reward Model acts as a judge by assigning a single numerical score to evaluate how good or bad the AI's response is.

exercises.prompt: Which of these AI systems behaves like a Reward Model?

options.text: System A: "Input: 'Write a poem' -> Output: 'The stars shine bright in the quiet night...'"
System B: "Input: 'The stars shine bright' -> Output: Score: +0.95"
System C: "Input: 'The stars shine bright' -> Output: 'Las estrellas brillan con fuerza'"

exercises.explain: System B represents a Reward Model because it evaluates a generated text and outputs a score rating its quality, rather than writing the text itself.

exercises.prompt: You are designing a chatbot system. Which of the following tasks is specifically handled by the Reward Model?

options.text: Generating multiple creative draft responses for the user to choose from
Translating user prompts into multiple foreign languages simultaneously
Evaluating an AI's response and outputting a single score representing its quality
Chatting directly with the user to ask clarifying questions about their prompt

exercises.explain: The Reward Model's sole job is to evaluate a response generated by another AI and output a single score indicating how good, helpful, or safe that response is. It does not generate the text itself.
